title: Canistrum minutum
taxonomy: Canistrum minutum belongs to the family Bromeliaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as bromeliads. This family is one of the largest in the flowering plant kingdom, with over 3,000 species. The genus Canistrum is a group of epiphytic plants native to the Americas, with Canistrum minutum being one of the species within this genus. The classification of Canistrum minutum also places it within the order Poales, a group of monocot flowering plants. The classification of Canistrum minutum is further supported by molecular phylogenetic studies that have confirmed its position within the Bromeliaceae family.

intro: Canistrum minutum, also known as the "Miniature Canistrum," is a small, epiphytic plant species belonging to the Bromeliad family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain, fight off infections, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Canistrum minutum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Canistrum minutum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly employed to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
